route.name: Henry Coe Trial by Fire Overnighter
route.region: Henry W. Coe State Park / Morgan Hill
route.description: Public GPX Bay Area-adjacent overnighter with steep park roads/trails, rugged fire-road riding, and water/camp constraints.
route.surfaceMix: Public GPX Bay Area-adjacent overnighter with steep park roads/trails, rugged fire-road riding, and water/camp constraints.
route.bestSeason: Spring/fall are friendlier; July requires heat, water, and fire-restriction screening.

difficultyProfile.surface: Public GPX Bay Area-adjacent overnighter with steep park roads/trails, rugged fire-road riding, and water/camp constraints.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: Water availability inside Henry Coe is seasonal and must be verified source-by-source.
resources.food: No route resupply; carry all food from Morgan Hill/South Bay.
resources.camping: Henry Coe camping/backcountry rules and reservations/permits must be verified for the exact date.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before ride-ready promotion.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

tips: Good California-priority candidate only after park water/camp rules are current.

verification.notes: Public GPX imported and parsed locally. Final legal/resource checks still control ride-ready promotion.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Route line is imported from public GPX and suitable for planning review.

review.decision: reject_rework; strong local near-miss, but first-come backpacking permit, exact bike-legal GPX audit, July 10-13 forecast/smoke, and water-currentness block ride_ready
blockers: California State Parks warns Henry Coe summers are hot and dry, often above 90 F.
The official page says finding water during summer months can be difficult.
Henry Coe is not ride-ready for next weekend until a specific summer water plan and heat/fire go/no-go threshold are set.
Water/camp/legal gates cannot be cleared until the exact route track and intended camps are verified against official park rules.
Weather heat gate blocked until NWS point forecast covers July 10-13.
AQI/smoke not verified for ride dates.
No GPX-level confirmation that every Henry Coe trail/road segment is bicycle-legal.
Wood or charcoal campfires are prohibited for wildfire season; gas stove only if cooking.
Official park page warns summer water can be difficult to find and highs often exceed 90 F.
Cannot promote ride_ready because July 10-13 NWS point forecast, AQI/smoke, and any day-of park/fire closures are not source-backed yet.
Backpacking permit for Pacheco Camp cannot be reserved; legal mechanics are first-come first-served, so campsite access is not guaranteed until permit is issued at Hunting Hollow/Coe Ranch/Dowdy.
Official park guidance warns summer highs often exceed 90 F and finding summer water can be difficult; current water observations are good but some backups are older than 30 days.
Wood/charcoal campfires are prohibited for wildfire season; gas stove only with extreme caution.
Legal-access pass depends on not using closed trails and on no recent >0.5 inch rain triggering the 48-hour singletrack bike closure.
No exact online proof clears every GPX trail/road segment as bike-legal under current Henry Coe rules.
Backpacking permit and Pacheco Camp cannot be reserved or guaranteed for July 10-13.
Exact July 10-13 heat, fire-weather, and smoke/AQI forecast cannot be verified on July 2.
Summer water is not proven current enough for ride-ready use; some backups are older than 30 days.
Wildfire-season restriction means stove-only cooking; no wood or charcoal fires.
requiredFixes: Check exact forecast for Morgan Hill/Henry Coe and choose early starts or reject if too hot.
Verify park water sources, overnight rules, and fire restrictions for July 10-13.
Confirm bike-legal trail/road sequence against Henry Coe rules.
Confirm July 11 campsite/reservation or legal backpacking itinerary.
Verify each route segment is bicycle-legal on current Henry Coe maps/rules.
Set a conservative heat cutoff and water capacity plan before any promotion.
Verify exact legal campsites/permits and water points against the official park map/rules.
Recheck NWS point forecast and CAL FIRE/park fire restrictions no earlier than July 7.
Check AirNow/fire.airnow.gov on July 9 and ride mornings.
Check the exact GPX trail sequence against current Henry Coe bicycle rules/map.
Confirm legal overnight campsite or backpacking permit plan for July 10-13.
Final-refresh park restrictions, forecast, smoke/AQI, and water-source status within 24-48 hours of departure.
On July 8-9, refresh Coe water map for Pacheco Camp trough, Live Oak Spring/Trough, Kelly Lake, and Coit Horse Camp; reject if Pacheco/Live Oak system is not YES.
On July 8-9, check NWS point forecast for Henry Coe/Pacheco Camp/Kelly Lake corridor and reject if highs or heat risk exceed group threshold.
On July 9 and ride mornings, check AirNow/fire.airnow.gov and official park/current restrictions for smoke, closures, red flag/fire-weather restrictions, and singletrack rain closures.
At Hunting Hollow before riding, obtain the first-come backpacking permit naming the intended legal overnight area; if unavailable, do not ride the overnighter as planned.
Carry the current Henry Coe map and avoid Corral Trail, Spring Trail, Forest Trail, Frog Lake Trail, Madrone Soda Springs Trail, Mile Trail, and Orestimba Wilderness.
Obtain the current Henry Coe bicycle handout/map and verify the GPX segment-by-segment.
Before riding, obtain the first-come backpacking permit at Hunting Hollow/authorized station; cancel or convert to day ride if unavailable.
Refresh official park restrictions, NWS forecast, CAL FIRE incidents, AirNow/fire.airnow.gov smoke, and Coe water feed within 24-48 hours of departure.
Add a bailout cue sheet covering Hunting Hollow, Coit Road return, Kaiser-Aetna/Dowdy option, emergency contacts, and no-cell assumptions.
Carry current Henry Coe map and avoid Orestimba Wilderness, Corral Trail, Spring Trail, Forest Trail, Frog Lake Trail, Madrone Soda Springs Trail, and Mile Trail.
openQuestions: What changed in the July 9-10 final refresh for closures, weather, smoke, water, and camps?

promotionChecklist.targetWindowNote: Close-to-home GPX-backed option, but July heat and summer water difficulty block ride-ready promotion until checked source-by-source.
promotionChecklist.officialGeometryNote: Local GPX/GeoJSON artifacts exist for the 36.1 mi two-day route with route waypoints for Hunting Hollow, Pacheco Camp, Live Oak Spring, Pacheco Falls, Kelly Lake, Coit Springs/Grapevine, and return.
promotionChecklist.campingPermitsNote: Pacheco Camp is legal only through first-come Henry Coe backpacking permit mechanics; no source proves a specific July 10-13 permit will be held.
promotionChecklist.closuresFireWeatherNote: Wood/charcoal campfires are banned for wildfire season; gas stoves are permitted with extreme caution. July 10-13 point forecast/AQI/fire-closure status is not source-backed yet, and official park text warns summer highs often exceed 90 F.
promotionChecklist.legalAccessNote: Official Henry Coe biking rules say 200+ miles of roads/trails are open to mountain bikes; GPX-named corridor does not name closed trails and stays outside Orestimba, but final use depends on avoiding closed trails and no 48-hour rain-triggered singletrack closure.
promotionChecklist.waterResupplyNote: Live Coe water JSON shows current route-corridor water observations, including Pacheco Camp trough by road YES on 2026-06-27, but official water page says water can change quickly and must be refreshed within 48 hours.

days.name: Henry Coe Trial by Fire Overnighter segment 1
days.camp: Henry Coe camping/backcountry rules and reservations/permits must be verified for the exact date.

days.name: Henry Coe Trial by Fire Overnighter segment 2

waypoints.name: Paved road from here to Hunting Hollow

logistics: California State Parks Henry W. Coe page lists bike trails, drinking water, camping facilities, and park context. https://www.parks.ca.gov/?page_id=561
California State Parks warns hot, dry summers bring highs above 90 F and that finding water during summer months can be difficult. https://www.parks.ca.gov/?page_id=561
California State Parks notes Class 1 e-bikes are allowed only where bicycle riding is permitted and some areas are closed to bicycles. https://www.parks.ca.gov/?page_id=561
California State Parks Henry W. Coe source checked July 2, 2026: https://www.parks.ca.gov/?page_id=561

Proposed Henry Coe itinerary: July 10 Hunting Hollow start after self-registration/backpacking permit and overnight vehicle fee, camp at Pacheco Camp only if permit is issued; July 11 return via Live Oak Spring/Pacheco Falls/Kelly Lake/Coit corridor.
Draft water plan: start with all needed water from off-park source, carry filter plus chemical backup, and do not start unless Pacheco Camp/Live Oak system remains YES within 48 hours of departure.

readinessEvidence.finding: Official page lists drive-in, hike-in/group, backpacking, and hike/bike overnight fee mechanics plus ReserveCalifornia/call-in reservation path for named sites.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks Henry W. Coe page

readinessEvidence.finding: Official page says wood or charcoal campfires are not allowed for the remainder of wildfire season as of June 12, 2026; gas stoves are permitted with caution.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks Henry W. Coe page

readinessEvidence.finding: Official page lists bike trails and says Class 1 e-bikes are allowed where bicycle riding is permitted, but some areas are closed to bicycles.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks Henry W. Coe page

readinessEvidence.finding: Current restrictions checked July 2; Coe Ranch and Hunting Hollow entrances always open and reservation availability YES for July 10-13.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks Henry W. Coe

readinessEvidence.finding: Official park source confirms bicycle trails exist, but the GPX corridor still needs trail-by-trail confirmation against bike-legal routes and bicycle-closed areas.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks Henry W. Coe

readinessEvidence.finding: Wood or charcoal campfires are prohibited as of 2026-06-12 for the remainder of wildfire season; gas stoves are allowed with caution. Official park page warns summer water can be difficult to find and highs often exceed 90 F.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks Henry W. Coe

readinessEvidence.finding: Official Henry Coe biking rules say 200+ miles of roads/trails are open to mountain bikes, but bikes are not allowed in Orestimba Wilderness or on Corral Trail, Spring Trail, Forest Trail, Frog Lake Trail, Madrone Soda Springs Trail, and Mile Trail; singletrack closes for 48 hours after more than 0.5 inch rain.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Henry W. Coe State Park Mountain Biking

readinessEvidence.finding: Backpacking permits are required and first-come first-served, issued at Coe Ranch/Hunting Hollow/Dowdy when open; Pacheco Camp permit cannot be treated as held before arrival.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Henry W. Coe State Park backpacking/camping rules

readinessEvidence.finding: Live Coe water observations include Pacheco Camp trough by road YES on 2026-06-27, 100% level, flow 3.0; Pacheco Camp tank YES on 2026-05-05; Live Oak Spring/Trough YES on 2026-04-04; Kelly Lake YES on 2026-05-05; Coit Horse Camp Trough #1 YES on 2026-05-31.
Bikepacking.com publishes a downloadable GPX and local GPX/GeoJSON artifacts exist.
Official park rules confirm many bike-legal roads/trails but also list bike-closed trails; no online proof cleared every GPX segment against the current official bicycle handout/map.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks Henry W. Coe rules

readinessEvidence.finding: Backpacking permits are required and first-come, first-served at Coe Ranch, Hunting Hollow, or Dowdy; Pacheco Camp cannot be reserved or proven available for July 10-13.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks Henry W. Coe camping/backpacking information

readinessEvidence.finding: Pacheco Camp water is promising from June observations, but backups are mixed/older and State Parks warns summer water can be difficult; exact July 10-13 reliability cannot be cleared now.
Wood/charcoal fires are banned for wildfire season and July 10-13 NWS heat/fire-weather plus AirNow smoke/AQI cannot be verified from July 2.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: NWS, CAL FIRE, AirNow, and California State Parks

not ride-ready; official park availability is promising but route source mismatch, water, heat, and AQI gates block promotion
